purpose: Define how CALI uses R:\ as a machine-learning-adjacent offline cache, retrieval layer, and knowledge distillation substrate.
primary_goals: Maintain local research corpora relevant to machine learning and AI.
Build embeddings and vector indexes for fast semantic retrieval.
Support knowledge distillation, semantic clustering, and pattern extraction.
Preserve fallback access when external sources are unavailable or unstable.
resilience_benefits: works when APIs are offline or rate-limited
legal_and_operational_posture: Prefer public, open, and research-friendly sources.
Respect source terms even when data is locally cached.
Keep provenance in manifests and registry exports.
Use snapshots before large refreshes when practical.
initial_build_queue: Create machine_learning subfolders under datasets, embeddings, indexes, vector_indexes, snapshots, and registry_exports.
Add source manifests for scholarly metadata, model registries, benchmark tables, and patent metadata.
Define chunking and embedding policy for long-form documents.
Create crosswalk tables between tasks, datasets, models, and benchmark families.
success_signals: CALI can answer machine learning research questions from local storage first.
Embeddings and vector indexes cover the highest-value machine learning corpora.
Model, dataset, and benchmark references are cross-linked.
Offline retrieval remains useful even when no network source is available.
